---
title: "Installing Python Packages"
---

How you install Python packages depends on whether you are using a **conda environment**. See our page on [conda environments](/pages/resources/conda.qmd) to learn how conda can help with **version control** and **reproducibility**. If you’re working within a conda environment, follow the conda installation instructions. Otherwise, you can install packages using **pip** with `pip install`.

For instance, to install **kb-python**, you may use:

### Conda
```bash
conda install -c conda-forge -c bioconda kb-python
```

### pip
```bash
pip install kb-python
```

## Installing JupyterLab
JupyterLab is an interactive environment for writing Python scripts. It is especially useful for data analysis workflows. You may also install JupyterLab with pip or Conda:

### pip
```bash
pip install jupyterlab
```

### Conda
```bash
conda install -c conda-forge jupyterlab
```

Once installed, you can open it with:
```bash
jupyter lab
```
